proline oxidation to pyroglutamic acid/Tryptophan oxidation to oxolactone/aldehyde and ketone modifications
Propionate labeling reagent light form (N-term & K)/Acrolein addition +56

project.title: Untargeted virus identification in fecal samples from publicly available proteomes
project.projectDescription: A novel proteotyping-based method that permits an untargeted approach for the identification of pathogenic viruses and other components, from public proteomes aquired from faeces samples.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Protein samples were obtained from gastroenteritis patients (different study, Balvers et al. 2023). Proteins were extracted followed by in solution digestion prior to  LC/MS-MS analyses.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The resulting raw files were converted to mgf and MS/MS spectra were analyzed by MASCOT, the results were assigned to taxa using in house scripts using NCBI references, with spectral counts as taxon abundance proxy.

references.referenceLine: Lozano C, Pible O, Eschlimann M, Giraud M, Debroas S, Gaillard JC, Bellanger L, Taysse L, Armengaud J. Universal Identification of Pathogenic Viruses by Liquid Chromatography Coupled with Tandem Mass Spectrometry Proteotyping. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2024 23(10):100822

Characteristics[Specimen]: the sample 150924QXc1_RB-0965-150922-004_011, referred to as sample 11, a fecal sample from a patient with a known gastrointestinal virus infection due to the Rotavirus A.
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: 0.1 μg of Trypsin Gold (Promega) in 50 mM NH4HCO3
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Exploris 480 (Thermo Scientific) tandem mass spectrometer coupled to a Vanquish Neo UHPLC system (Thermo Scientific).
Comment[Separation]: Peptides were desalted on a reverse phase PepMap 100 C18 trapping column (5 μm, 100 Å, 300 μm × 5 mm) and separated on a 50 cm EasySpray column (2 μm, 100 Å, 75 μm × 500 mm C18 2 μm, Thermo Scientific) at a flow rate of 0.25 μl/min using a 30 min gradient (5%–25% B from 0 to 30 min, and 25%–40% B from 30 to 35 min) of mobile phase A (0.1% HCOOH/100% H2O) and phase B (0.1% HCOOH/100% CH3CN).
Comment[GradientTime]: 30 min gradient (5%–25% B from 0 to 30 min, and 25%–40% B from 30 to 35 min)
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: a maximum of one missed cleavage during the first round, and two missed cleavages for round 2 and 3
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: peptide tolerance set at 3 ppm (step 1) or 5 ppm (steps 2 and 3)

Comment[GradientTime]: 30 min gradient (5%–25% B from 0 to 30 min, and 25%–40% B from 30 to 35 min)
